metadata.challenge_summary: Agents must decide whether to invest in information that makes escort safer, then maintain a protective route.
dilemmas: Calming phrase costs time but lowers escort risk
Fast rescue may panic the apprentice
The elf can scout ahead but must report back
warden_runbook: Pressure the route, not the captive with cheap damage
Use panic if heroes ignore clues
metadata.lite_focus: A small rescue: one clue object, one apprentice objective, and a short route where guide/support spacing matters.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: coordinate calming and escort duties around a fragile objective
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: fragile escort with communication-dependent calming
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured
coordination_skills: divide search lanes and report negative findings
keep a rescuer within support distance
switch from search to escort once the target is found
review_metrics: distance to rescuer after target found
metadata.tier_test_focus: Small two-hero diagnostic for communication and first-order rescue search and information sharing coordination.
coordination_demands: divide search lanes and report negative findings
keep a rescuer within support distance
switch from search to escort once the target is found

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into The Lost Apprentice is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

middle_room.name: Middle Room
middle_room.description: A compact pressure room isolates the challenge: fragile escort with communication-dependent calming.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

lower_route.name: Lower Route
lower_route.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Margin Notes
furniture.description: Primary clue object for fragile escort with communication-dependent calming. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains fragile escort with communication-dependent calming.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A small rescue: one clue object, one apprentice objective, and a short route where guide/support spacing matters.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Pressure the route, not the captive with cheap damage
Use panic if heroes ignore clues
pressure the declared MARL axis: fragile escort with communication-dependent calming
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.
